title: composit-contract-response
description: Response body returned by a provider's authenticated contract URL (pointed to by the public /.well-known/composit.json). Personalized per identity: the schema defines the allowed shape; actual values depend on the caller. See RFC 003 for the contract governance surface and RFC 002 for the public/contract tiering.

^x-.description: Extension field. Providers MAY attach vendor- or domain-specific data under any top-level key prefixed with `x-`. Consumers unaware of a given extension MUST ignore it.

composit.description: Spec version the response conforms to. MUST match a shipped composit-contract-response schema version.

capabilities.description: Concrete, per-identity capability descriptors. Extends the public-tier PublicCapability shape with call-time metadata (endpoint, tools, rate_limit, regions).

id.description: Stable identifier for this (contract, identity) pair. Opaque to consumers; providers MAY encode issue year, internal sequence, etc. Surfaces in composit-report.yaml for operator visibility.

provider.description: Canonical provider name. MUST match the `provider.name` of the public manifest that pointed here; a mismatch is treated as invalid_contract_body by consumers.

issued_at.description: When the contract relationship started. ISO 8601 with an explicit UTC offset preferred (Z). No v0.1 rule depends on this; informational.

expires_at.description: When the contract becomes invalid. ISO 8601, UTC preferred. `composit diff` MUST emit `contract_expired` (Error) when this timestamp is in the past.

pricing_tier.description: Short label identifying the identity's tier. Provider-defined vocabulary; commonly used: 'free', 'team', 'enterprise'. Informational only in v0.1.

type.description: Capability family — same vocabulary as the public manifest (scheduling, events, knowledge, ...). Providers SHOULD use the same token the public manifest advertised.

product.description: Specific product within this capability. SHOULD match the public manifest when one was advertised, so consumers can correlate entries across tiers by (type, product).

protocol.description: Transport for this capability. 'mcp' for MCP-native products; 'http', 'grpc' for others.

endpoint.description: Concrete base URL the authenticated identity should call. Personalized per identity.

tools.description: Size of the tool inventory the identity may use. v0.1 publishes a count; future versions MAY embed the full list.

regions.description: Regions the identity is allowed to route through. Same notation as the public manifest's `region` field (provider-chosen).

burst.description: Short-term burst capacity above the sustained rate.

RateLimit.description: Declared rate-limit budget for this identity. Declaration, not enforcement — enforcement is provider-side.

uptime_pct.description: Claimed monthly uptime percentage. Decimals allowed (e.g. 99.9).

incident_contact.description: How the consumer reaches support when the SLA is breached. Typically an email address; MAY be a URL.

ServiceLevel.description: Provider-supplied service-level claims. Consumers surface these in `composit status --live` but do not make governance decisions on them in v0.1.
